title: memstead-plugin/v1 binding
description: A Binding is one versioned record per source→mem obligation: the declaration (`intent`, `source_facets`, `reference_mems`, `destination_mem`, `deny_paths`, `coverage_semantics`, `rules`) plus an `operations { build, sync, verify }` block. It collapses the retired projection + ingest pair into a single file at `.memstead/projections/<mem>/<name>.json`. `source_facets` reference facets by name under the binding's own mem; `reference_mems` supply read-only cross-mem context; `destination_mem` is the mem the binding writes into. Every operation is optional: an absent build/sync makes that mutating operation refuse at run time with a `projection enable <op>` remedy; an absent verify means engine defaults (verify is read-only). The `prune` block, when present, requests a deletion-proposal guarantee validated against the source medium at binding time.

version.description: Binding format version. v1 bindings carry the integer 1; a file without it is refused by the loader.

intent.description: What the binding is trying to accomplish — prose for the agent. Optional.

source_facets.description: Facets (by name, resolved under the binding's own mem) the binding consumes.

reference_mems.description: Read-only reference mems (by name) that supply cross-mem context.

destination_mem.description: The mem this binding writes into.

deny_paths.description: Paths excluded from the binding's scope (workspace-relative globs). Strategy-invariant — moved up from the per-ingest record. A glob deny list is legal only over a path-shaped medium namespace (codebase / filesystem / git); the engine refuses it at binding-validation time over a graph or web medium.

coverage_semantics.description: Whether the binding claims to cover everything in its declared scope (`exhaustive`) or a deliberately partial slice (`curated`). Defaults to `exhaustive` when absent.

rules.description: Free-form binding rules (e.g. a one-shot lens `routing` string). Opaque to the engine — consumed only by the one-shot brief renderer.

prune.description: Prune policy — additive, optional. Absent means prune is disabled (no deletion proposals). Present means prune produces deletion proposals in the sync brief under the requested guarantee. Prune has no independent schedule: it rides the sync brief, so it carries no trigger/batch_size.

guarantee.description: The guarantee the binding requests, validated against the medium's base-leg retrievability at binding-validation time. `never-clobber` is a full three-way merge, supportable only where the source base leg is retrievable (git-backed sources); `conflict-flag` (the default) presents both sides and never auto-writes over a model-side edit.

operations.description: The operations this binding declares. Every operation is optional; an absent mutating operation (build/sync) refuses at run time with a remedy, an absent verify means engine defaults.

buildOperation.description: The build operation — the only operation carrying a mode. Grows new coverage (`discovery`) or runs a single bounded pass (`one-shot`). trigger / batch_size / post_actions are scheduling attributes.

mode.description: `discovery` builds out new coverage; `one-shot` runs a single bounded pass. The retired `refinement` value is not accepted.

post_actions.description: Free-form post-run actions (e.g. a one-shot `archive_source` flag). Opaque to the engine — consumed only by the one-shot brief renderer.

archive_source.description: When true, the one-shot brief instructs the agent to archive the source mem after every destination is processed.

syncOperation.description: The sync operation — the sole maintenance writer. Carries no mode. An absent sync block makes that mutating operation refuse at run time.

verifyOperation.description: The verify operation — read-only measurement. Carries no mode. adjudication_cap and full_resync_every are additive tier-3 scheduling knobs that default to the engine's dogfood-tuned values when absent.

adjudication_cap.description: The maximum number of hash-drift adjudications a single verify run asserts before queueing the remainder as backlog. 0 disables the cap.

full_resync_every.description: Every N verify runs, a full-enumeration coverage sweep runs (for enumerable mediums). 0 disables scheduled full walks.

trigger.description: What sets an operation running. `loop` runs continuously under `/loop`; `manual` runs only when invoked; `on-event` is reserved for event-driven hooks.

batchSize.description: How many artifacts a single run processes.
